asset_name: BPC-157 Growth Hormone Receptor Expression in Rat Tendon Fibroblasts

primary_source.title: Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 Enhances the Growth Hormone Receptor Expression in Tendon Fibroblasts

model: Primary rat Achilles tendon fibroblast culture
key_findings: Growth hormone receptor expression increased in a dose- and time-dependent pattern at mRNA and protein levels.
The paper reported up to approximately sevenfold increase in growth hormone receptor expression by day 3 in the described experimental system.
Growth hormone added after BPC-157 pretreatment increased viable cell number and PCNA expression.
Growth hormone stimulation increased phosphorylated JAK2 in BPC-157-pretreated tendon fibroblasts.
limitations: Mechanistic findings do not establish clinical tendon-healing efficacy.
The experimental system cannot reproduce the full multicellular environment of an injured tendon.
